---
title: "Sales Trend Analysis"
description: "Analysing monthly sales trends and visualising performance across product categories."
author: "Meghana"
date: "2025-02-10"
image: ../../images/project2.png
categories: [Python, Sales, Data Analysis, Pandas]
---

## Overview

This project analyses simulated monthly sales data to identify trends 
and visualise performance across product categories.

## Libraries Used

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `pandas` | Data manipulation |
| `numpy` | Generating sample data |
| `plotly.express` | Interactive visualisations |

## Analysis

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import numpy as np

# Generate sample sales data
np.random.seed(42)
months = pd.date_range(start="2024-01-01", periods=12, freq="ME")
categories = ["Electronics", "Clothing", "Food"]

data = []
for cat in categories:
    sales = np.random.randint(5000, 20000, size=12)
    for month, sale in zip(months, sales):
        data.append({"Month": month, "Category": cat, "Sales": sale})

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Line chart
fig = px.line(
    df,
    x="Month",
    y="Sales",
    color="Category",
    title="Monthly Sales by Category (2024)",
    markers=True
)
fig.show()
```

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
Electronics showed the highest variance month-to-month, suggesting 
seasonal demand patterns worth investigating further.
:::

- **Electronics** showed the highest sales overall.
- **Food** remained the most consistent category throughout the year.
- **Clothing** peaked mid-year, likely due to seasonal demand.
